channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Son sentenced to 22 years' jail over arson murder of his mother
item.description: Shannon James Doble will remain behind bars until at least 2040 for the "callous" attack on his 65-year-old mother, who died after receiving burns to 90 per cent of her body.
item.dc:creator: Melinda Hayter

media:group.media:description: Shannon James Doble pleaded guilty to his mother's murder in September last year.

item.title: Police officers accused of alleged assault of mentally ill prisoner
item.description: Two Queensland police officers are contesting the charges that relate to a 2024 incident at a regional watch house, arguing that investigators added things to the alleged victim's statement that he never said.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: Jeffrey Hunter KC (left), defence barrister for Senior Constable Ulrich Weiss, and David Jones KC (second from left), counsel for Senior Constable Andrew Couchman, leave the Cairns courthouse with solicitors.

item.title: Snow, rain, icy winds forecast as polar blast hits south-east Australia
item.description: A wintry mix of snow, icy temperatures, and showers is set to hit Australia's south-east, bringing a sharp end to the recent run of warm autumn weather.
item.dc:creator: Tyne Logan

media:group.media:description: There have already been several instances of snow this year in Tasmania, but this is likely to be the lowest snow for many.

item.title: Finnish defence giant has tech 'Australia might be interested in'
item.description: The company that provides much of Australia's satellite surveillance for real-time disaster management may also soon be sharing its defence technology, as Finland becomes the latest European country to seek closer ties on security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Finnish trade minister Ville Tavio is the latest to visit Australia from the EU to boost defence and trade ties.

item.title: Fire destroys SES headquarters and new $270K flood rescue vehicle
item.description: The SES is mopping up after a fire ripped through its Central Coast headquarters, destroying millions of dollars' worth of specialist equipment.
item.dc:creator: Mary-Louise Vince and Sarah Forster

media:group.media:description: The fire destroyed millions of dollars' worth of equipment, including emergency response vehicles.

item.title: NT budget faces criticism over cuts and lack of cost of living relief
item.description: The Country Liberal Party's second NT budget has received a lukewarm response from civil society groups, with advocates criticising funding cuts and a "missed opportunity" to support Territorians.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Bill Yan and Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro look at 2026-27 budget papers.

item.title: Car locks disabled at hospital as signal interference sparks security concern
item.description: Radio interference from nearby cranes is identified as the reason visitors to Bunbury Hospital have been unable to lock their cars.
item.dc:creator: Madigan Landry and Chela Williams

media:group.media:description: Visitors to the hospital campus have reported issues with car locks failing to engage when parked at the site.

item.title: Canberra man sentenced for knowingly exposing woman to genital herpes
item.description: In one of the first cases of its kind in Australia, a man with a sexually transmitted infection admits to recklessly inflicting grievous bodily harm.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Daniel Marriott was sentenced in the ACT Supreme Court.

item.title: Australia gives $30m to Fiji to help fuel prices in Pacific nation
item.description: The Australian government says the funding will help Fiji combat rising fuel prices and support its role as a regional fuel hub.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Penny Wong announced the fuel funding after meeting with Fiji's leader Sitiveni Rabuka.

item.title: River camp residents ask people 'not to judge' them after baby's death
item.description: Long-term campers set up at the base of a levee popular with walkers are asking people to have an "open mind" about why they are there, saying many have nowhere else to go.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Pezet

media:group.media:description: Many of the shelters have been set up for several months.

item.title: Adelaide 36ers coach quits a month on from dramatic championship loss
item.description: Mike Wells steps down from his role as Adelaide 36ers head coach, saying he wants to spend more time with his family in the US.

media:group.media:description: Mike Wells has been the 36ers' head coach for two years.

item.title: Accused Christmas Day killer told friend 'I murdered my father', court hears
item.description: A man charged with murdering his father at a retirement village in Adelaide's south on Christmas Day in 2022 sent a message to a friend saying he had committed the crime, a court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: David Eldridge allegedly murdered his father, Robert Eldridge, on Christmas Day 2022.

item.title: Melbourne teacher Kim Ramchen pleads guilty to stabbing principal
item.description: A Melbourne court has been told a school principal who was ambushed by a knife-wielding teacher remains too traumatised to return to work.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Aaron Sykes has not returned to his role as principal since the attack.

item.title: Blues hand bullocking Rooster Origin debut as Maroons keep cards close
item.description: Queensland opts to name a squad, rather than a final line-up, for Women's State of Origin II, with NSW expected to hand a debut to powerful Roosters forward Rima Butler.

media:group.media:description: Rima Butler is one of nine Roosters in the NSW squad.

item.title: Fears for crumbling regional roads despite $1b in Victorian budget
item.description: The Victorian budget includes $1 billion to fix the state's crumbling regional roads, but the budget papers reveal fewer country road repairs are expected over the coming financial year.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Stuchbery

media:group.media:description: More than $1 billion has been directed towards upgrading regional and rural roads in the 2026 Victorian budget.

item.title: Cancellation 'disaster' for business that expected Inland Rail for 25 years
item.description: Regional communities from Queensland to Victoria react after the major federal rail project was axed overnight.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus and Andrew Mangelsdorf

media:group.media:description: The federal government has cancelled the remainder of what would have been the country's biggest rail project in a century.

item.title: Child protection workers stood down over Kumanjayi Little Baby reports
item.description: Three child protection workers have been stood down and a department-wide review ordered over its handling of a series of child protection reports about Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.dc:creator: Jason Walls

media:group.media:description: Robyn Cahill says she can't talk about the specifics of the case.

item.title: Australia's emergency fuel stockpile to increase under multi-billion dollar plan
item.description: More than $10 billion will be spent bolstering Australia's fuel security as the federal government attempts to shield the nation from the long-term consequences of the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Borys

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese has unveiled plans for a national fuel security package.

item.title: Tim Picton's alleged attacker avoids immediate jail over separate charge
item.description: Brodie Jake Dewar had admitted assaulting another man two days before he allegedly struck Mr Picton, who later died in hospital.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: Brodie Dewar arrives at court for a sentencing hearing after pleading guilty to assaulting a man in Kalamunda.

item.title: 'Justice is served': Afghan women's footballers react to FIFA ruling
item.description: A FIFA rule change has allowed the Afghan women's football team to compete in official internationals for the first time since the Taliban took over in 2021. Mina Ahmadi says the victory is a "very emotional" one for the team.
item.dc:creator: Kate O'Halloran and Daniela Intili

media:group.media:description: Mina Ahmadi played in the friendly series last year, and is now excited to play in official matches.

item.title: 'Don't know life without fishing': SA algal bloom causes ban extension
item.description: One of South Australia's main fishing zones will remain closed for another year to allow stocks to recover from the algal bloom, sparking concerns from fishers over their future and warnings of price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Peta Doherty

media:group.media:description: Gulf St Vincent commercial fishers, like Bart Butson, are facing another year of restrictions.

item.title: Qld government backs proposal for zip-line over Brisbane River
item.description: A Queensland developer has proposed to build a wheelchair-accessible zip-line to cross the Brisbane River from Kangaroo Point to the City Botanic Gardens.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of the proposed zip-line across the Brisbane River.

item.title: Jury retires in case of triple-fatal Greyhound bus crash driver
item.description: A jury retires to consider its verdict in the trial of a driver of a Greyhound bus which caused a triple-fatal crash on the Bruce Highway in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: The jury in the trial of a Greyhound bus driver involved in a triple-fatal crash has retired.

item.title: More than 500 turtles discovered on the outskirts of far west NSW town
item.description: Hundreds of turtles are thriving near an outback NSW town, only months after the lake where they live was on the verge of drying up.
item.dc:creator: Lily McCure

media:group.media:description: Over 500 turtles have been found at Imperial Lakes Nature Park near Broken Hill.

item.title: Cruise of a lifetime becomes nightmare after hantavirus outbreak
item.description: It was meant to be the journey of a lifetime for Boston travel influencer Jake Rosmarin, crossing the south Atlantic Ocean on the MV Hondius, but the trip has turned into a nightmare after three people died in a hantavirus outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: Jake Rosmarin made a tearful statement regarding his experiences on board the MV Hondius to an Instagram audience of 51,000 followers.
